description: Aggregate per-sample Salmon quant.sf files to a gene-level integer counts matrix via pytximport, using counts_from_abundance='length_scaled_tpm' (the right choice for gene-level DE) and rounding the estimated counts to integers as PyDESeq2 requires. Writes counts.csv (genes x samples) and metadata_template.csv.
rules: local analysis — reads quant output, writes counts.csv + metadata_template.csv under record_store
estimated (non-integer) Salmon counts are rounded to integers; never emits TPM/FPKM
requires a tx2gene map (transcript_id -> gene_id); discovers <sample>/quant.sf, falling back to flat *.sf
needs python3 + pandas + pytximport (uv pip install pytximport); without pytximport the porter degrades gracefully and still records an audit line
usage: Set QUANT_DIR (dir of per-sample <sample>/quant.sf) and TX2GENE (transcript->gene map TSV/CSV). Output: counts/counts.csv + counts/metadata_template.csv.
limitation: Salmon mode only. pytximport must be installed for real counts; offline (lib absent) it produces an empty placeholder so the contract still holds.
